data.title: setup-claude-secrets.sh:93 - Replace grep with --json --jq for secret filtering
data.description:
## Violation: JSON-First Principle

**File**: `scripts/setup-claude-secrets.sh`
**Line**: 93

### Current Code (Anti-Pattern)

```bash
gh secret list --org "$ORG" | grep CLAUDE
```

### Problem

Using `grep` to filter GitHub CLI output violates the JSON-first principle. This parses unstructured text instead of using structured JSON data with proper filtering.

### Recommended Fix

```bash
gh secret list --org "$ORG" --json name --jq '.[] | select(.name | contains("CLAUDE"))'
```

### Benefits

* ✅ Uses structured JSON output with `--json`
* ✅ Filters with native `--jq` instead of post-processing
* ✅ More reliable (doesn't depend on text formatting)
* ✅ Returns properly formatted data

### Reference

See project gh documentation standards, specifically the anti-patterns section that shows "hundreds of wrong attempts" using grep/cut/awk.

**Priority**: Medium
**Type**: Tech Debt
**Standards**: JSON-First, No Text Parsing
